purpose: Score based on HOW the lead interacted — channel patterns, frequency, recency, and follow-up behavior.

features.reason: Number of WhatsApp interactions — WhatsApp-heavy leads show stronger informal engagement
Number of phone calls — voice calls signal higher commitment and urgency
Number of email interactions — email-heavy leads are often formal/enterprise type
Total touchpoint volume — high count means both sides are invested
The primary channel used — channel preference reveals lead persona and behavior type
Channel used for first contact — first channel sets the engagement tone
Channel used for most recent contact — last channel shows current preferred communication mode
Whether lead engaged across multiple channels — multi-channel = stronger intent
How many times the lead switched channels — channel-switching shows determination to get a response
Total hours spent in conversations — longer engagement = deeper relationship
Bucketed conversation time (high_fast / low_slow / medium_medium) — categorical grouping helps LGBM handle nonlinear patterns
How recently the last message was sent (fresh/active/stale/cold) — stale leads drop off sharply in conversion rate
Composite depth-of-engagement score — captures quality of engagement beyond raw counts
Interaction of dominant channel + current pipeline stage — combined feature capturing stage-channel dynamics
Past deal success — historical context anchors the interaction score in real outcomes
How many follow-up rounds were attempted — higher sequence = either very engaged or very stubborn
Whether the lead responded to any follow-up — response to follow-up is the strongest re-engagement signal
Rate of follow-up responses — normalized across leads with different interaction volumes
Type of last follow-up sent (QUOTE_FOLLOWUP, SALES_FOLLOWUP, etc.) — reflects current deal stage in follow-up lifecycle
Agent responsiveness ratio — consistent agent engagement keeps leads from going cold
